sn3.10:1.0.1: Now on that occasion a great mass of people had been put in bondage by King Pasenadi of Kosala—some with ropes, some with clogs, some with chains.sn.i.77
sn3.10:1.0.2: Then, in the morning, a number of bhikkhus dressed … and said to the Blessed One: "Here, venerable sir, a great mass of people have been put in bondage by King Pasenadi of Kosala, some with ropes, some with clogs, some with chains."
sn3.10:1.0.3: Then the Blessed One, having understood the meaning of this, on that occasion recited these verses:
sn3.10:1.0.4: v.397"That bond, the wise say, is not strongMade of iron, wood, or rope;But infatuation with jewellery and earrings,Anxious concern for wives and children
sn3.10:1.0.5: v.398This, the wise say, is the strong bond,Degrading, supple, hard to escape.But even this they cut and wander forth,Unconcerned, having abandoned sensual pleasures."
